title: Elaphoglossum pygmaeum
uses: Elaphoglossum pygmaeum is a species of epiphytic fern that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. However, further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. More studies are needed to explore the full range of medicinal uses of Elaphoglossum pygmaeum.
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum pygmaeum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Elaphoglossum, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. The species is classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Elaphoglossum pygmaeum is further classified as a member of the domain Eukaryota. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.
